title: Nyla's First Day Friends
synopsis: Nyla feels scared on her first day at preschool, but when she meets the classroom pets - a gentle rabbit, cheerful fish, and friendly hamster - she discovers that making new friends can turn scary feelings into happy ones.

pages.text: Nyla walked into her new preschool classroom. Her dark curls bounced as she looked around. Everything seemed so big and new! She felt scared and held Mama's hand tight. "I don't know anyone here," Nyla whispered softly.

illustration.description: A young Black girl with beautiful dark curls wearing a bright yellow dress stands nervously in a colorful preschool classroom, holding her mother's hand. Her brown eyes look worried but curious. The classroom has rainbow decorations, small tables, and book corners with warm lighting. The style is cheerful cartoon with bold primary colors and rounded, friendly shapes against a textured background.
illustration.alt: Nyla, a young Black girl with curly hair, looks nervous while holding her mother's hand in a bright preschool classroom

pages.text: Then Nyla saw something wonderful! In the corner was a big cage with a fluffy white rabbit. "Hello there," said Miss Chen, her teacher. "This is Snowball. Would you like to meet him?" The rabbit's pink nose twitched in a friendly way.

illustration.description: Nyla approaches a large, clean cage containing a fluffy white rabbit with pink ears and a twitching pink nose. Miss Chen, an Asian woman with kind eyes and a warm smile, kneels beside them. The rabbit looks gentle and welcoming. Bright morning sunlight streams through windows, creating a cozy atmosphere with soft blues and greens complementing the white rabbit.
illustration.alt: Nyla and her teacher Miss Chen looking at Snowball the white rabbit in his cage

pages.text: Nyla's scared feelings started to melt away. She gently petted Snowball's soft fur. "He's so fluffy!" she giggled. Near the window, colorful fish swam in a big tank. A tiny hamster ran on his wheel. "The animals live here at school," Miss Chen explained. "They love meeting new friends like you!"

illustration.description: Nyla carefully pets the white rabbit through the cage bars, her face now showing joy and wonder instead of fear. Behind them, a large fish tank with bright tropical fish and a hamster cage with a brown hamster are visible. Other diverse children play happily in the background. The scene is filled with warm oranges, blues, and greens, showing a welcoming, safe environment.
illustration.alt: Nyla happily petting Snowball while other classroom pets and children are visible in the background

pages.text: Soon, other children came to see the pets too. There was Marcus with his bright smile, Sofia with her red backpack, and quiet Aiden who loved the fish. "Want to help me feed them?" asked Marcus. Nyla felt happy now. She had found her first friends - both furry and human ones! School wasn't scary anymore.

illustration.description: Nyla stands confidently with three other diverse children around the pet area. Marcus, a Latino boy with a big smile, holds fish food. Sofia, a girl with braids and a red backpack, points excitedly at the fish tank. Aiden, a quiet Asian boy, watches the fish swim. All the children look happy and engaged. The illustration shows friendship and inclusion with bright, warm colors and everyone's faces showing joy and acceptance.
illustration.alt: Nyla and her new friends Marcus, Sofia, and Aiden gathered around the classroom pets, all looking happy and excited

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Nyla feel when she first came to school?
questions.explanation: Nyla felt scared because everything was new and she didn't know anyone yet.

questions.question: What was the rabbit's name?
questions.explanation: The white rabbit's name was Snowball because he was fluffy and white like snow.

questions.question: How did Nyla feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Nyla felt happy because she made new friends and wasn't scared anymore.

questions.question: Who helped Nyla feel better about school?
questions.explanation: Both the classroom pets and the other children helped Nyla feel welcome and make friends.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.text: Nyla felt _____ on her first day, but the fluffy white _____ helped her feel better.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: The children helped feed the pets and Nyla felt _____ because she made new _____.
